title: The LORD's wrath and goodness
summary_text: Nahum opens by announcing that the LORD is both jealous in judgment and good as a stronghold for those who trust him. The oracle moves from divine power over the sea and mountains to a promise that Assyria's oppression will end. Judah hears not only warning, but also good news that the yoke will be broken.
key_points: God is presented as both avenging and protective.
The LORD controls sea, storm, and mountains.
Assyria's oppression will not last forever.
Judah receives a message of peace.
evidence_phrases: stronghold in the day of trouble

title: Nineveh's defenses collapse
summary_text: The attacker is already at the gates as Nahum describes shields, chariots, and panic in the streets of Nineveh. The city that once feared no rival is now told to gather its strength and watch it fail. The chapter paints the collapse of the empire in vivid, immediate images.
key_points: The lion's den is emptied and burned.
evidence_phrases: the shield of his mighty men

title: Woe to the bloody city
summary_text: Nahum finishes with a direct woe against Nineveh, calling it a city of blood, lies, and endless violence. The prophet compares it to No-Amon to show that even great cities can fall when God acts against them. The chapter closes with no healing for Assyria's wound, only public ruin.
key_points: Nineveh is condemned for bloodshed and deceit.
Its power is compared to fallen No-Amon.
Foreign nations mock and abandon it.
The wound is fatal and beyond repair.
